title: Operating Systems & Computer Networks (JNTUK R20)
description: JNTUK R20 Year 2-3 — Complete coverage of Operating Systems (process management, memory, file systems) and Computer Networks (OSI/TCP model, protocols, security) aligned to JNTUK B.Tech CSE R20 syllabus.

modules.title: Getting Started
modules.description: Introduction and overview of Operating Systems & Computer Networks (JNTUK R20)

pages.title: OS & Networks — Course Overview
OS & Networks — Wiki Index

modules.title: Core Lectures
modules.description: All lectures and concept pages for this course

pages.title: Introduction to OS: Goals, Types, System Calls, OS Structure
Process Management: Process States, PCB, Context Switch, Threads vs Processes
CPU Scheduling: FCFS, SJF, Priority, Round Robin, Multilevel Queue
Process Synchronization: Race Conditions, Mutex, Semaphores, Monitors
Deadlocks: Conditions, Prevention, Avoidance (Banker's Algorithm), Detection
Memory Management: Paging, Segmentation, Virtual Memory, Page Replacement
File Systems: FAT, NTFS, Ext4, Inodes, Directory Structures, Disk Scheduling
Network Fundamentals: Types, Topologies, OSI vs TCP/IP Model, Transmission Media
Network Layer: IP Addressing, Subnetting, CIDR, Routing Algorithms (Dijkstra, DVR)
Transport Layer: TCP vs UDP, 3-Way Handshake, Flow Control, Congestion Control
Application Layer Protocols: HTTP/HTTPS, DNS, SMTP, FTP, DHCP
Network Security: Cryptography, SSL/TLS, Firewalls, VPN, Common Attacks
Wireless Networks: WiFi (802.11), Bluetooth, 4G/5G, Mobile IP

modules.title: Practice
modules.description: Practice, quizzes, coding, and project pages

pages.title: OS Lab: CPU Scheduling and Page Replacement Algorithm Simulations
Networks Lab: Socket Programming, Wireshark Analysis, Ping/Traceroute
Placement Guide: OS and Networks Interview Q&A for JNTUK Students
